dataset.source: v4 T5 with do_sample=True, top_p=0.9, temperature=1.0, num_return_sequences=2

conclusion: Sampled v4 T5 decoding adds noise faster than diversity. Each anchor gets 2 samples that are surface-different but ~10% of them are subtly wrong (top_p=0.9, T=1.0 lets the model occasionally drop a negation or pick a wrong subject). This contradicts the polarity-preservation that motivated v4 in the first place and the contrastive head ends up trained on partially-incorrect supervision. LogiQA's multi-step deductive chains amplify the noise -> near-random 29.3%.
